At some point between [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]] and [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], a '''battle''' of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] took place in the [[Sagma system/Legends|Sagma system]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories']]<ref name="TEA" /> [[Illisurevimurasi sector/Legends|Illisurevimurasi sector]].<ref name="EAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> The engagement pitted the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] against the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]], with the former emerging victorious.<ref name="TEA" />

==Behind the scenes==
The origins of the battle lie with a mention of the "[[Battle of Sagma]]"—the aftermath of which saw the Galactic Empire make [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Ganig]] and [[Sergeant/Legends|Sergeant]] [[Stecker]] famous heroes—in "[[Picutorion Viewed From the Top]]," a short companion narrative included in the [[1989]] ''[[Imperial Sourcebook]]'' that was written by [[Greg Gorden]] and published by [[West End Games]] for use with ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="ISB">{{WEGCite|story=Picutorion Viewed From the Top|book=Imperial Sourcebook}}</ref> ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'', a [[2009]] reference book by [[Daniel Wallace]] and [[Jason Fry]], subsequently noted a battle in the Sagma [[Star system/Legends|system]] on a map of Galactic Civil War engagements taking place between 2 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]] and 4 ABY.<ref name="TEA" />

[[File:Impsourcebook.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The origins of the Sagma system battle lie with the ''Imperial Sourcebook''.]]
On that map, the Sagma system battle was placed spatially close to Galactic Civil War battles in the [[Praadost system/Legends|Praadost]], [[Doniphon system/Legends|Doniphon]], and [[Telos system/Legends|Telos systems]].<ref name="TEA" /> Fry's [[Wizards of the Coast]] [[Star Wars Miniatures|''Star Wars'' Miniatures]] scenario series ''[[The Hunt Within: Valance's Tale]]'', published on [[Wizards.com]] between [[December 2]], [[2004]]<ref name="THW1">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=swminis/article/huntwithin1|text=The Hunt Within: Valance's Tale: Part 1|int=The Hunt Within: Valance's Tale: Part 1}}</ref> and [[January 27]], [[2005]],<ref name="THWC">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=swminis/article/huntwithin5|text=The Hunt Within: Valance's Tale: Conclusion|int=The Hunt Within: Valance's Tale: Conclusion}}</ref> had previously established that the Imperial campaign known as the [[Kwymar Suppressions/Legends|Kwymar Suppressions]]—preceded by the [[pacification of Praadost II]]—included engagements on the celestial bodies [[Assault on Doniphon|Doniphon]] and [[Battle of Telos IV (Kwymar Suppressions)|Telos]]<ref name="THW1" /> as well as the [[Raid on Picutorion|battle]] on [[Picutorion/Legends|Picutorion]]<ref name="THW1" /><ref name="THWC" /> which, per "Picutorion Viewed From the Top," took place after the Battle of Sagma.<ref name="ISB" /> In addition, ''The Hunt Within: Valance's Tale'' established that the Imperial [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] [[Imperial officer/Legends|officer]] [[Beilert Valance/Legends|Beilert Valance]] had participated in the Picutorion battle before being wounded and fitted with [[Cybernetics/Legends|cybernetic]] prosthetic parts.<ref name="THW1" />

However, the [[2012]] reference book ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'', authored by Fry and [[Paul R. Urquhart]], introduced a "Battle of Sagma" in which the Empire was victorious and that was set in [[18 BBY/Legends|18 BBY]].<ref name="EGTW">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref> Furthermore, a section was created for the book in which Valance's fitting with cybernetics was dated to [[17 BBY/Legends|17 BBY]]. Although the section was cut from ''The Essential Guide to Warfare'', it was later released in [[2014]] as part of [[Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare Author's Cut|''Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare'' Author's Cut]], a series of articles published on the [[StarWars.com]] [[Star Wars Blog|Blog]] by Fry and<ref name="Author's Cut">{{SW|url=news/the-essential-guide-to-warfare-authors-cut-part-11-serving-the-empire|series=Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare Author's Cut|text=Part 11: Serving the Empire}}</ref> [[Del Rey]] editor<ref name="Celestials">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-guide-to-warfare-authors-cut-the-celestials|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare'' Author's Cut — The Celestials|int=Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare Author's Cut}}</ref> [[Erich Schoeneweiss]] containing various cut content from the book. Thereby, the Picutorion battle was also dated to approximately 17 BBY, and the Galactic Civil War battle in the Sagma system from ''The Essential Atlas'' was [[Retroactive continuity|retroactively]] established to have been a separate engagement from the Battle of Sagma from "Picutorion Viewed From the Top."<ref name="Author's Cut" />
